title: When exactly WAS Shinji tanged
wikitext:
[]: When exactly WAS Shinji tanged?             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 24, 2007 11:48 am I've seen a handful of posts floating around that state Shinji was tanged way earlier than I imagined... I had thought he was actually one of the last to go. I didn't see any idication that Shinji had been tanged until after Eva 01 had been taken into GNR, and after we hear that popping sound Shinji immediately starts hearing everyone else. Am I right and have minsinterpreted some posts, or am I totally off the mark here?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Nov 24, 2007 12:20 pm I always thought it occurred when Kaworu appeared before him with the lines "is this better?" - which explains why Shinji goes on that trip thereafter - but isn't he shown physically sitting in the plug much later into the movie? He also gets tanged in ep. #19.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 24, 2007 12:23 pm Zugzwang wrote: I always thought it occurred when Kaworu appeared before him with the lines "is this better?" - which explains why Shinji goes on that trip thereafter - but isn't he shown physically sitting in the plug much later into the movie?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Nov 24, 2007 12:37 pm Mmm yes, maybe it has something to do with Eva 01's position as the Tree of Souls when it comes into contact with the lance (the chronology of the movie is completely messed up in my head and I can't remember when that occurs) - maybe in that position you don't need to get tanged to be a part of instrumentality.

[]: Re: When exactly WAS Shinji tanged?             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Nov 24, 2007 12:44 pm Semisubtle wrote: I've seen a handful of posts floating around that state Shinji was tanged way earlier than I imagined... I had thought he was actually one of the last to go. I didn't see any idication that Shinji had been tanged until after Eva 01 had been taken into GNR, and after we hear that popping sound Shinji immediately starts hearing everyone else. Am I right and have minsinterpreted some posts, or am I totally off the mark here?

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 24, 2007 1:02 pm But if that's the case, why do we see him as he is in the entry plug later on in the movie, and what is the 'popping' sound shortly after that if it's not Shinji reverting to LCL? It's only after the pop that he both hears and sees the images that flash on the screen, like he's just become fully aware of the merged minds around him.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Nov 24, 2007 3:52 pm The popping sound when the exit sign is seen is a slap. Which instance of entry plug are you referring to (that isn't heavily stylized by effects)?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Nov 24, 2007 4:34 pm I think the shots of Shinji within the plug in #26' are there for cinematic reasons, i.e. to show him as aware of and reacting to his surroundings... ...but I don't think that is intended to nullify the various earlier indications that he had lost physical form within the plug. Compare the above with this cut from #20 (when he is definitely still disembodied): Note the red coloring of both scenes, which is probably a visual convention indicating that what we are seeing is intended as a representation of non-physical reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Nov 24, 2007 4:38 pm Man! I was just about to post that. I remembered Ritsuko saying something about a "image of his mind still being present in the plug" at that point in the Weaving A Story episode.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Nov 24, 2007 5:06 pm I thought it was his soul and conciousness.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 24, 2007 5:42 pm Shin-seiki wrote: I think the shots of Shinji within the plug in #26' are there for cinematic reasons, i.e. to show him as aware of and reacting to his surroundings... <SNIP>

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Nov 24, 2007 5:48 pm Ornette wrote: The popping sound when the exit sign is seen is a slap.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sat Nov 24, 2007 6:15 pm Ornette wrote: The popping sound when the exit sign is seen is a slap.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 24, 2007 9:51 pm Screenies, yay! (Why post the screenies? Because they're there. ) Episode 25: Shinji: What is this sensation? I feel like I've experienced it before, as if the shape of my body is melting away. It feels so good. I feel like I'm growing, expanding outward...On and on... *Cue Shinji fading away* Episode 26': Yui: This Rei is from your soul. It's an embodiment of your wish. Rei (off): What do you wish for? (Boobies! Just, erhm, not ON him. ^^; ) TANGED! (Oh, that's rough.) *Cue EoTV/EoE Instrumentality water droplet thingy*

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 26, 2007 12:47 am when i first saw a post saying that Shinji was tanged, i totally "WTF!!!'d" but then when i started thinking about it, it made sense that Shinji wouldve been tanged when kaworu showed up and Shinji was all having his little orgasm. but i guess i was wrong i should probably watch EoE again

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:00 pm Semisubtle wrote: Ornette wrote: The popping sound when the exit sign is seen is a slap.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:04 pm Reichu wrote: [url]http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-m26/m26_C381_slap.gif[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 03, 2007 1:18 am chee wrote: Reichu wrote: [url]http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-m26/m26_C381_slap.gif[/url]
